name: SMILE Digital Twin Advisor — Hospital Operations
description: A domain-specific AI agent that solves real hospital operations problems (bed utilisation, equipment downtime, energy waste, patient readmission) by routing through all 7 LPI MCP tools with a multi-step reasoning loop, then synthesising results with a local Ollama LLM into a structured SMILE implementation roadmap. Every claim is cited to the specific LPI tool that provided it. Includes full provenance tracking and all 3 easter eggs found and documented.

skills.name: Bed Utilisation Digital Twin Roadmap
skills.description: Takes a hospital bed management / patient flow problem and returns a phased SMILE roadmap. Starts from impact KPIs (occupancy variance, discharge prediction accuracy), maps the patient flow ecosystem in Phase 1, defines a MVT for a subset of high-variance wards in Phase 2, and builds towards a real-time discharge prediction twin.
examples: Surgical ward at 98% occupancy while medical wards run at 60%
No real-time visibility of patient flow or predicted discharge dates

skills.name: Equipment Predictive Maintenance Roadmap
skills.description: Maps MRI, CT, ventilator, or other clinical device downtime problems to SMILE phases. Key insight: failure mode ontology (Phase 3) must precede AI (Phase 5). Returns a roadmap from equipment Reality Canvas through ontology creation to a condition-based maintenance twin.
examples: MRI downtime 10 unplanned hours per week, calendar-based maintenance only
CT scanner failing without warning, engineers reactive not proactive

skills.name: Hospital Energy Digital Twin Roadmap
skills.description: Applies SMILE to hospital energy problems — HVAC on fixed schedules, no room-level visibility, net-zero targets with no baseline. Returns a roadmap from Reality Canvas of energy flows through edge-native contextual intelligence for occupancy-aware HVAC control.
examples: Energy costs up 38%, HVAC on fixed weekly schedule regardless of occupancy
No room-level energy measurement, board net-zero target for 2030

reasoning.description: Step 1: SMILE overview. Step 2: list_topics to discover KB areas. Step 3: title-boosted first knowledge search. Step 4: refined knowledge search based on step 3 findings. Step 5: domain-matched case studies. Step 6: scenario-specific insights. Step 7: phase deep-dive. Step 8: MVT methodology steps.
reasoning.refinement_loop: query_knowledge called twice — second call uses terms extracted from first result

explainability.title_boost_exploit: _title_boost_query() prepends domain nouns matching KB entry titles verbatim
